ocr.text: In Table 1, the model classifications are organized into two categories: "0.5" and "Blood flow" and "Insertion depth (mm)." For
the "Reduced" model labeled as "Low," the values are 390.2 for Blood flow and 516.2 and 593.1 for the Insertion depth. The
"High" model features values of 295.3 for Blood flow, with corresponding Insertion depths of 434.6 and 544.4. The "Full"
model under "Low" has Blood flow at 302.3 and Insertion depths of 408.7 and 550.7, while the "High" model shows Blood
flow at 290.5 and Insertion depths of 400.7 and 526.8. In Table 2, the epigenetic mechanisms are categorized under "Epigenetic
mechanism" and "Epigenetic factors." The first mechanism, "DNA (de)methylation and hydroxymethylation," includes factors
such as DNA methyltransferase enzymes, Methyl-CpG-binding domain proteins, DNA excision repair proteins, Cytidine
deaminase enzymes, Gadd45 proteins, and Ten-Eleven Translocation enzymes. The second mechanism, "Histone and chromatin
modifications," lists factors including Histone modifying enzymes (e.g., histone [de]acetylase and [de]methylase), Polycomb
group proteins, Trithorax group proteins, and REST and CoREST epigenetic regulatory complexes.
Model  Blood flow  Insertion depth (mm)
Reduced  Low  390.2  516.2  593.1
Epigenetic mechanism  Epigenetic factors

DNA (de)methylation and hydroxymethylation  DNA methyltransferase enzymesMethyl-CpG-binding domain proteinsDNA excision repair proteinsCytidine deaminase enzymesGadd45 proteinsTen-Eleven Translocation enzymes

Histone and chromatin modifications  Histone modifying enzymes (e.g., histone [de]acetylase and [de]methylase)Polycomb group proteinsTrithorax group proteinsREST and CoREST epigenetic regulatory complexes
Non-coding RNAs (ncRNAs)  Short-interfering RNAsMicroRNAsPIWI-interacting RNAsSmall nucleolar RNAsLong ncRNAsLong intergenic ncRNAs
RNA editing  Adenosine deaminase that act on RNA enzymesApolipoprotein B editing catalytic subunit enzymes
Nuclear organization  Nuclear domains (e.g., nuclear speckles)Nuclear lamina (e.g., lamin genes)
The third mechanism, "Non-coding RNAs (ncRNAs)," includes short-interfering RNAs, MicroRNAs, PIWI-interacting RNAs,
Small nucleolar RNAs, Long ncRNAs, and Long intergenic ncRNAs. The "RNA editing" mechanism includes Adenosine
deaminase that act on RNA enzymes and Apolipoprotein B editing catalytic subunit enzymes. The final mechanism, "Nuclear
organization," encompasses Nuclear domains (e.g., nuclear speckles) and Nuclear lamina (e.g., lamin genes).
